summary: Luxembourg's posture is set by the Ministry of State, whose national quantum technologies strategy charts a roadmap toward secure quantum networks and treats post-quantum cryptography as a quantum-resistant solution that can be integrated with existing systems. The government's digital-trust portal, run by ILNAS, has opened a public consultation on the European Union roadmap for the transition to post-quantum cryptography, which recommends that critical use cases migrate by the end of 2030 at the latest.
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
govActors:
Ministry of State | Luxembourg Ministry of State, issues the national quantum technologies strategy and roadmap
ILNAS | Luxembourg Institute for Standardisation, Accreditation, Safety and Quality of products and services, runs the digital-trust portal and the consultation on the EU post-quantum roadmap
Government of Luxembourg | publishes the national quantum communication roadmap factsheet (Phase 1 2025-2027, Phase 2 2027-2030, Phase 3)
provenance: Ministry of State lead, strategy and roadmap, PQC as integrable quantum-resistant solution: Luxembourg's Quantum Technologies Strategy 2024 (ingested, T1)

Phased roadmap (Phase 1 2025-2027, Phase 2 2027-2030, Phase 3): Quantum Communication in Luxembourg factsheet (ingested, T1); PQC reference not explicit in the snippet, flagged for review
migrationTimeline 2030 milestone is an EU roadmap recommendation that Luxembourg notes, not a Luxembourg national deadline - REVIEW
targetCompletion Phased: roadmap describes phases without a terminal date for the cryptographic transition
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp (NIST/ETSI/ISO) named as national guidance in captured summaries
algorithms/standardFamilies omitted: not stated in captured summaries
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / taker, High): EU coordinated roadmap (NIS Cooperation Group); adopts the NIST baseline as a taker. No national algorithm.

obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2; migration follows the EU coordinated 2026/2030/2035 timeline. No distinct national post-quantum instrument is recorded in the Atlas sources.
